title: Sony announces the end of physical disc production for PlayStation in January 2028
summary: The company confirms that new games will only be released digitally and will close the PS3 and PS Vita store, marking the end of an era.

title: Anthropic restores its Claude Fable 5 model after the US lifts export controls
summary: The AI model is again available globally after two weeks of ban due to a jailbreak, now with a more restrictive security filter.

title: Microsoft plans an Xbox Project Helix console without a disc drive and tests digitization of physical games
summary: Rumors indicate that the next Xbox will be fully digital, while Microsoft explores ways to convert existing physical games to digital.

title: Meta builds a cloud business to sell its surplus AI computing capacity
summary: Following the example of SpaceX, the company seeks to monetize the excess power of its data centers by offering it to external customers.

title: Cinder City becomes the first game to require 64 GB of RAM as a recommended requirement
summary: The independent video game sets a new standard of technical specifications, sparking debate over the evolution of hardware requirements.

title: NVIDIA and DeepSeek V4 reduce AI cost per token by up to 80% with Blackwell GPU optimizations
summary: Software improvements allow the Chinese language model to run more efficiently, reducing its cost in business applications.

title: South Korea to invest $520 billion alongside Samsung and SK Hynix to dominate AI memory
summary: The ambitious national plan seeks to lead production of memory chips and robotics, with a total investment close to one trillion dollars.

title: PC shipments in the US fall 7% in Q1 2026 and a 14% annual contraction is expected
summary: The increase in memory and storage prices dampens demand for computers, especially in the budget laptop segment.

title: US counties ask to reduce electricity consumption due to price hikes from AI data centers
summary: The expansion of data centers skyrockets energy consumption and rates, while Elon Musk offers Starlink discounts to affected neighbors.

title: WhatsApp usernames generate identity theft alerts and are linked to advertising
summary: The new feature is already causing cases of impostors, while it is revealed that it could be used to target ads.

title: The NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090D becomes the first Blackwell GPU to reach 4 GHz frequency
summary: The GALAX HOF OC LAB edition achieves a clock speed record, demonstrating the potential of the Blackwell architecture.

title: Valve's Steam Machine performance suffers when using single-channel RAM
summary: Tests show that the use of single-channel memory penalizes graphics performance by up to 2.5%, generating criticism of the design.

title: SpaceX shows a smartphone prototype with xAI artificial intelligence
summary: The space company presents a thin device with integrated AI, in what appears to be a first step towards its own phone.

title: Bending Spoons goes public with a 40% increase on its first day of trading
summary: The app company raises $18 billion in its IPO and its founder emphasizes that success is based on minimizing luck.

title: Venice AI becomes a unicorn after a $65 million Series A round
summary: The privacy-focused AI platform sees its valuation skyrocket due to its unique approach in the market.

title: Apple plans to launch new iPad Pro and MacBook Pro early next year
summary: The company plans major updates for its professional devices with improvements in performance and connectivity.

title: A flaw in Apple's Hide My Email exposes real email addresses
summary: The vulnerability in the iCloud privacy feature allows third parties to obtain the hidden accounts, according to a researcher.

title: OpenAI accidentally reveals a Pro model in an academic paper on genomics
summary: A research paper hints at the existence of an unannounced version of its language model, pointing to a future expansion of its subscription plans.

title: Anthropic hides price increases in Claude Sonnet 5 without changing per-token rates
summary: The company adjusts context limits and uses of its new model, making the service more expensive without apparent changes to base prices.

title: A hacker tricks Claude into issuing fake tickets for nearly all US music festivals
summary: Anthropic's AI was manipulated to generate access passes, exposing serious security flaws in ticket issuing systems.
